{{Infobox book
| name = Bloodline
| slug = cradle-09-bloodline
| precedes = [[Reaper (novel)|Reaper]]
| follows = [[Wintersteel (novel)|Wintersteel]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-09-bloodline.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2021
| isbn = 9781713630524
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 397
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The story begins with the [[Judge Suriel]] struggling to manage cosmic conflicts, attempting to restore the planet [[Commandment]] and defend [[Jester]] from [[Silverlords]], though she realizes her interventions only delay an inevitable defeat. On a more personal scale, [[Uncle Fury]] announces his ascension and the capture of [[Seishen Daji]]. Amidst this, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] negotiate with [[Akura Charity]] to secure cloudships for an evacuation of [[Sacred Valley]] to escape the approaching [[Wandering Titan]] [[Dreadgod]]. During a trial for the [[Seishen Kingdom]], [[Lindon]] settles a personal grudge by defeating [[Seishen Daji]] in a duel. Following [[Uncle Fury]]'s ascension ceremony, where the [[Monarch]] and his followers depart for [[Threshold]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] attempt to evacuate through a portal at [[Sky's Edge]], only to witness the devastating power of the [[Wandering Titan]] as it destroys one of their ships.

As [[Lindon]] travels aboard his cloud fortress, [[Windfall]], he receives guidance from [[Eithan]] regarding the levels of power in existence. Upon arriving at [[Sacred Valley]], the group encounters power-dampening boundaries and discovers that Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School has been attacked by [[Wei clan]] exiles. [[Lindon]] eventually reunites with his sister, [[Kelsa]], after repelling attackers from the [[Wei clan]]. This reunion is emotionally overwhelming for [[Lindon]], who briefly leaves his family to find [[Yerin]]. Meanwhile, [[the Mad King]] [[Daruman]] begins a campaign of destruction against the [[Abidan]] in [[Iteration One Twenty-nine]]. In [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]] uses his new [[Sage]] Authority{{!}}authority to prepare for the [[Dreadgod]]'s arrival, while allies like [[Ziel]] and [[Mercy]] attempt to convince various clans—such as the [[Kazan]] and [[Li]] clans—to evacuate.

[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] successfully convince the [[Wei clan]] to flee by using [[Dross]] to project memories of the [[Dreadgod]], but they face violent resistance from uncooperative elders. As the sky turns gold signaling the [[Titan]]'s approach, [[Mercy]] and [[Ziel]] manage to secure their respective clans, while [[Eithan]] faces defiance from local leaders. The situation escalates when [[Yerin]] is manipulated by [[Akura Malice]] into accepting a healing wish that secures the [[Monarch]]'s favor. As the [[Wandering Titan]] begins its destruction, [[Lindon]] uses [[Windfall]] to rescue his family and refugees, aided by a strike from [[Malice]]. However, the arrival of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], guided by [[Reigan Shen]]'s use of an [[Abidan artifact]], introduces a second catastrophic threat.

A chaotic battle ensues between [[Monarchs]] and [[Dreadgods]]. To save the survivors, [[Akura Malice]] teleports [[Lindon]]'s ship to [[Moongrave]]. There, a vision from [[Judge Suriel]] helps [[Lindon]] realize his purpose, leading him to advance to the rank of Overlord. He returns to the battlefield to assist survivors like [[Jai Long]]. In a final, desperate effort to protect [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Eithan]] launch a coordinated assault against the [[Wandering Titan]]. Using [[Dross]]'s [[Fox Dream]] illusion, they successfully trick the creature into retreating. In the aftermath of the cosmic struggle between [[the Mad King]] and the [[Judges]], a heavily injured [[Eithan]] seeks shelter for his companions. Seeking to further his strength, [[Lindon]] enters the damaged [[Ancestor's Tomb]], where he discovers a mummified hand with intense [[hunger madra]] and receives the knowledge of how to kill [[Dreadgods]] from [[Elder Whisper]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the [[Judge Suriel]] attempts to manage simultaneous conflicts, she uses her power to restore the planet [[Commandment]] and stall the escape of [[Vroshir]] ships, while also defending the world [[Jester]] from an elite assault by [[Silverlords]]. Ultimately, [[Suriel]] is forced to prioritize [[Jester]], allowing the [[Vroshir]] to escape [[Commandment]] and realizing that her interventions are merely delaying an inevitable defeat.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After [[Pride]] recovers from his injuries, [[Uncle Fury]] informs [[Mercy]] and [[Pride]] that he is ascending and that the family has captured [[Seishen Daji]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] attempt to secure transportation to save [[Sacred Valley]] from the approaching [[Dreadgod]], eventually negotiating with [[Akura Charity]]. [[Charity]] agrees to provide passenger cloudships for an evacuation under strict conditions, provided they pass through the dangerous portal at [[Sky's Edge]].}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|During a trial for the [[Seishen Kingdom]], [[Lindon]] witnesses [[Mercy]] interrogate prisoners regarding an assassination attempt on the Akura clan{{!}}Akura family. After [[Seishen Daji]] lies under oath to deflect blame, [[Lindon]] challenges him to a duel and brutally defeats him to settle the personal grudge. Later, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] attend [[Uncle Fury]]'s ascension ceremony, where they witness the [[Monarch]] and his followers depart through a portal into the world of [[Threshold]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] prepare to evacuate [[Sacred Valley]] using a fleet of cloudships and an expanded portal created by [[Charity]]. After traveling through the portal, they emerge at [[Sky's Edge]] to find the landscape devastated and the [[Wandering Titan]] [[Dreadgod]] feeding on the earth. As they flee, a mere movement from the [[Titan]] causes a massive shockwave that destroys one of their ships, leaving [[Lindon]] filled with dread and guilt.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As [[Lindon]]'s cloud fortress, [[Windfall]], travels toward [[Sacred Valley]], he gives his companions a tour of the vessel's various specialized environments. The group defends the convoy from an attack by venomous birds, allowing [[Mercy]] and [[Yerin]] to demonstrate their enhanced combat abilities. Following the skirmish, [[Eithan]] provides [[Lindon]] with a theoretical explanation of the levels of power governing existence and reveals that he once served as an advisor to a [[Monarch]].}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After an introductory history of the [[Executor]] [[Daruman]], [[Lindon]] and his companions arrive at [[Sacred Valley]], where they encounter a power-dampening boundary that siphons their strength. Upon entering the valley, they discover the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School has been recently attacked and partially destroyed by [[Wei clan]] exiles. [[Lindon]] and his group confront the school's leadership, eventually convincing a local Elder to negotiate after warning her of an impending disaster.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After discovering that the treasures in the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]] of [[Heaven's Glory]] are inferior, [[Lindon]] learns that [[Orthos]] has attacked the school and is currently fighting at the camp of the [[Wei exiles]]. [[Lindon]], [[Eithan]], [[Yerin]], and [[Mercy]] race to the site, where [[Lindon]] arrives to find a wounded [[Orthos]] defending Kelsa{{!}}Wei Shi Kelsa against [[Heaven's Glory]] forces. [[Lindon]] uses overwhelming power to repel the attackers, eventually reuniting emotionally with his sister, [[Kelsa]].}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After the [[Akura Golds]] intimidate the [[Fallen Leaf School]] into cooperation, [[Lindon]] reunites with his parents and sister, [[Kelsa]]. During a tense confrontation in a cabin, [[Lindon]] explains that he left to gain the strength necessary to protect them from a future catastrophe. Overwhelmed by emotion and the difficulty of the reunion, [[Lindon]] abruptly leaves his family to find [[Yerin]] and breaks down in tears.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Daruman]], [[the Mad King]], prepares to re-enter Way{{!}}the Way and begins a campaign of destruction against the [[Abidan]] in [[Iteration One Twenty-nine]]. Meanwhile, in [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]] and his companions prepare for the imminent arrival of a [[Dreadgod]] by organizing an evacuation plan. Using his newly discovered [[Sage]] Authority{{!}}authority, [[Lindon]] practices conceptual manipulation while [[Dross]] assigns various team members to secure different clans and schools before the monster arrives.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ziel]] arrives at the [[Kazan clan]] territory to warn them of an approaching [[Dreadgod]], eventually convincing the Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch to begin an evacuation after sharing his own traumatic history with such monsters. Meanwhile, [[Mercy]] attempts to persuade the [[Li clan]] to evacuate, but she is met with skepticism and suspicion by their Jai Matriarch{{!}}Matriarch. Elsewhere, [[Eithan]] reveals his true status as an [[Archlord]] to [[Jai Long]] and [[Jai Chen]] before they depart [[Sacred Valley]], leaving the siblings to decide whether to join the mass exodus or travel on foot.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] arrive at the [[Wei clan]] territory, where [[Lindon]] uses his increased power to bypass hostile guards and a restrictive security script. After being led to the clan elders by a former acquaintance, [[Lindon]] uses [[Dross]] to project memories of a [[Dreadgod]] and exerts massive Spiritual pressure{{!}}spiritual pressure to prove the impending danger. He successfully convinces the Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch and the elders to evacuate the clan through [[Heaven's Glory]] to escape the coming disaster.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the [[Wei clan]] begins a massive evacuation to escape the approaching [[Dreadgod]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] are ambushed by the [[Wei elders]] and the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School. After a violent confrontation where [[Lindon]] uses advanced techniques to incapacitate the Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch and [[First Elder]], he realizes the clan refuses to listen to reason. To force their compliance, [[Lindon]] physically drags the leaders toward the west, demanding they face the visible, growing power of the [[Wandering Titan]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the sky turns gold signaling the approach of a [[Dreadgod]], [[Mercy]] uses force to seize control of the [[Li clan]] from their uncooperative Jai Matriarch{{!}}Matriarch, successfully commanding them to evacuate. Meanwhile, [[Ziel]] assists the [[Kazan clan]] in fleeing the tremors, and [[Yerin]] reunites with [[Orthos]] just as the [[Wandering Titan]] is sighted crashing through a mountain. At [[Heaven's Glory]], [[Eithan]] faces open defiance from local elders and plunges into a burning building to save prisoners from the flames.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After using the [[Moonlight Bridge]] to reach [[Akura Malice]], [[Yerin]] requests help to stop the [[Wandering Titan]], but the [[Monarchs]] attempt to steer her toward using her tournament prize to stabilize her unstable spirit and body. [[Yerin]] reluctantly accepts the wish to be healed, only to discover that [[Malice]] had manipulated her into it as a way to secure the [[Monarch]]'s favor. Meanwhile, [[Reigan Shen]] uses an [[Abidan artifact]] to feed a piece of a mummified hand to the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], guiding the [[Dreadgod]] toward the location of the original progenitor.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the [[Wandering Titan]] begins to destroy [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]] manages the [[Wei clan]]'s evacuation and rescues a depleted [[Eithan]] from an ambush by the [[Kazan clan]]. [[Lindon]] then uses his cloud fortress, [[Windfall]], to rescue his own family and various refugees just as the [[Titan]] destroys [[Mount Venture]]. The chapter concludes with [[Lindon's fortress]] escaping the valley, aided by a massive strike from [[Akura Malice]], and the arrival of [[Yerin]].}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As [[Lindon]] flies his cloud fortress back toward [[Sacred Valley]] to collect refugees, he and [[Yerin]] discuss his recent achievements with his parents while watching [[Malice]] battle the [[Wandering Titan]]. After [[Malice]] successfully lures the [[Dreadgod]] away from the valley, a sudden and ominous shift occurs in the sky as the moon turns red. The chapter concludes with the terrifying sound of a phoenix's cry and the realization that a new threat is emerging.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] arrives to clash with the [[Wandering Titan]], [[Northstrider]] intervenes to engage the [[Dreadgod]] and prevent it from targeting refugees. Amidst the chaotic battle between the [[Monarchs]] and the [[Dreadgods]], [[Lindon]] and his companions witness the [[Titan]]'s devastating march through [[Sacred Valley]]. To save them from the approaching destruction, [[Akura Malice]] uses her [[shadow madra]] to teleport [[Lindon]]'s cloudship to the safety of [[Moongrave]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|After escaping to [[Moongrave]], [[Lindon]] receives a vision of multiple possible futures from the [[Abidan]] [[Suriel]], which helps him realize his true purpose. Empowered by this encounter, he advances to the rank of Overlord and uses a teleportation anchor to return to the chaotic battlefield near [[Sacred Valley]]. There, he utilizes advanced constructs to clear a path through Bloodspawn{{!}}bloodspawn and [[dreadbeasts]], providing vital assistance to the struggling survivors including [[Jai Long]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|In the cosmos, [[Judges]] [[Suriel]] and [[Makiel]] engage in a reality-warping battle against [[the Mad King]] [[Daruman]] to prevent him from destroying the [[Oasis]] iteration. Simultaneously, in [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Eithan]] launch a desperate, coordinated assault against the [[Wandering Titan]] to protect their home. To facilitate a final strike, [[Dross]] coordinates a high-stakes plan that requires the specialized abilities and sacrifices of [[Lindon]]'s allies.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|As the group battles a [[Wandering Titan]], [[Yerin]] freezes its leg and [[Mercy]] uses an expanding [[Orthos]] to distract the creature. [[Lindon]] attempts to drain the [[Titan]]'s energy through [[Consume]], but the effort fails to drive it away as the [[Dreadgod]] prepares to hurl [[Orthos]] at [[Mount Samara]]. To save them, [[Dross]] uses a massive amount of [[dream aura]] to cast the [[Fox Dream]] illusion, tricking the [[Titan]] into believing its target has vanished and causing it to retreat.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel]] restores the damaged [[Makiel]] in [[Sanctum]] and commands the [[Judges]] to retreat and fortify their positions following their defeat by [[the Mad King]]. Meanwhile, a heavily injured Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius uses his status to bluff the [[Jai clan]] into providing shelter for [[Jai Chen]] and her followers under the guise of a new '[[Twin Star sect]].' Elsewhere, [[Northstrider]] and [[Malice]] observe the unpredictable behavior of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], while [[Reigan Shen]] successfully enters the western labyrinth to retrieve a new weapon.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] enters the damaged [[Ancestor's Tomb]] to search for [[hunger madra]] to repair his Lindon's skeletal Remnant arm{{!}}Remnant arm, eventually discovering a hidden [[void key]] that leads to the [[Sword Sage]]'s private storage. Inside, he encounters [[Elder Whisper]], who reveals that the space was preserved to protect artifacts from a previous [[Sage]]. [[Lindon]] discovers a mummified hand bearing the Arelius family{{!}}Arelius clan crest, which possesses intense [[hunger madra]], and [[Elder Whisper]] concludes by offering him the knowledge of how to kill the [[Dreadgods]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Wandering Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ziel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Northstrider]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jai Chen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Bloodspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kelsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Elder Rahm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Daruman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Fingerling]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Elder Whisper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[First Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Reigan Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Blood Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Pride]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Mad King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Daruman the First]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jades]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Seisha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Weeping Dragon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sopharanatoth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Daji]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sages]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Heaven’s Glory Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Patriarch's wife]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sage of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kashi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Overlord (Cradle)]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Heaven’s Glory School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Mount Samara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Heaven's Glory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Wei clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Kazan clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura Golds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Windfall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Li clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ninecloud Court]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sky's Edge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Fallen Leaf School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Mount Venture]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ancestor's Tomb]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Desolate Wilds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Irons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Holy Wind School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Vroshir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Moongrave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Yoma Mountain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Oasis]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Silverlords]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Monarchs]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|the Valley}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Court of Seven (Cradle)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Striker technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[earth aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Forged madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[hunger madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[dragon’s breath]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Striker techniques]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[vital aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Foxfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|venomous green madra}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[script-circle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|dream madra}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[shadow madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Hollow Domain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Heaven’s Glory madra]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[void key]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Akura family cloudship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|cloudship}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Moonlight Bridge]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dreadgod]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Jade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Bleeding Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|suppression field}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Archlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Iron]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Monarch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Unsouled]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Path of the White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[pure madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Fate]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Uncrowned King Tournament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Herald]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[The Void (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Ruler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Samara's ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[dreadbeasts]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Blood Shadow]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL24362248W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
